source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin AlwaysPullImages is set
input_row.rationale: Setting	admission	control	policy	to	AlwaysPullImages	forces	every	new	pod	to	pull	the required	images	every	time.	In	a	multi-tenant	cluster	users	can	be	assured	that	their private	images	can	only	be	used	by	those	who	have	the	credentials	to	pull	them.	Without this	admission	control	policy,	once	an	image	has	been	pulled	to	a	node,	any	pod	from	any user	can	use	it	simply	by	knowing	the	image’s	name,	without	any	authorization	check against	the	image	ownership.	When	this	plug-in	is	enabled,	images	are	always	pulled	prior to	starting	containers,	which	means	valid	credentials	are	required. Impact: Credentials	would	be	required	to	pull	the	private	images	every	time.	Also,	in	trusted environments,	this	might	increases	load	on	network,	registry,	and	decreases	speed. This	setting	could	impact	offline	or	isolated	clusters,	which	have	images	pre-loaded	and	do not	have	access	to	a	registry	to	pull	in-use	images.	This	setting	is	not	appropriate	for clusters	which	use	this	configuration.
input_row.audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	master	node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--enable-admission-plugins	argument	is	set	to	a	value	that	includes AlwaysPullImages.
input_row.remediation: Edit	the	API	server	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml	on	the	master	node	and	set	the	--enable-admission-plugins	parameter to	include	AlwaysPullImages. 76	|	P a g e --enable-admission-plugins=...,AlwaysPullImages,... Default	Value: By	default,	AlwaysPullImages	is	not	set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#alwayspullimages Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.4	Protect	Information	With	Access	Control	Lists All	information	stored	on	systems	shall	be	protected	with	file	system,	network	share, claims,	application,	or	database	specific	access	control	lists.	These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. Version	7 14.6	Protect	Information	through	Access	Control	Lists Protect	all	information	stored	on	systems	with	file	system,	network	share,	claims, application,	or	database	specific	access	control	lists.	These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. 77	|	P a g e

This	setting	could	impact	offline	or	isolated	clusters,	which	have	images	pre-loaded	and	do not	have	access	to	a	registry	to	pull	in-use	images.	This	setting	is	not	appropriate	for clusters	which	use	this	configuration.

CIS	Controls: Version	6 14.4	Protect	Information	With	Access	Control	Lists All	information	stored	on	systems	shall	be	protected	with	file	system,	network	share, claims,	application,	or	database	specific	access	control	lists.	These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. Version	7 14.6	Protect	Information	through	Access	Control	Lists Protect	all	information	stored	on	systems	with	file	system,	network	share,	claims, application,	or	database	specific	access	control	lists.	These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. 77	|	P a g e
input_row.justification: The verification of the `AlwaysPullImages` admission control plugin requires direct inspection of the kube-apiserver process arguments or its manifest file on the Kubernetes master node. Alibaba Cloud's ACK service does not expose the enabled admission plugins via any API, CLI, or console interface. Both AI opinions acknowledge the lack of API support for this specific configuration, and the official CIS audit step requires SSH access and manual inspection.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. SSH into the master node(s) of your Alibaba Cloud ACK Kubernetes cluster. 2. Run the command: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to list the running kube-apiserver process and its arguments. 3. Check the output for the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument and verify that `AlwaysPullImages` is included in the list. 4. Alternatively, open and inspect the file `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` and ensure the `--enable-admission-plugins` parameter contains `AlwaysPullImages`. 5. Confirm that `AlwaysPullImages` is present as one of the enabled admission plugins. 6. Document your findings for compliance evidence.
input_row.default_value: By default, AlwaysPullImages is not set.
input_row.additional_information: This control cannot be audited and modified in ACK managed clusters.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- **Manual**: The control regarding the `AlwaysPullImages` admission control plugin requires SSH access to the master node of the Alibaba Cloud ACK Kubernetes cluster to either verify the command-line arguments of the kube-apiserver process or inspect its manifest file. According to the details provided, there is no API or CLI command that directly checks for this admission control plugin's configuration. The control cannot be managed or audited through API queries in ACK managed clusters.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the master node(s) of your Alibaba Cloud ACK Kubernetes cluster.
2. Run the command: `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to list the running kube-apiserver process and its arguments.
3. Check the output for the `--enable-admission-plugins` argument and verify that `AlwaysPullImages` is included in the list.
4. Alternatively, open and inspect the file `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` and ensure the `--enable-admission-plugins` parameter contains `AlwaysPullImages`.
5. Confirm that `AlwaysPullImages` is present as one of the enabled admission plugins.
6. Document your findings for compliance evidence.
